name: Muspelheim
description: Muspelheim, in Norse cosmology, is the primordial realm of fire and heat, situated in the south of Ginnungagap. It is the antithesis of Niflheim, the realm of ice and cold, and its fiery nature plays a crucial role in the creation of the cosmos. Ruled by the fire giant Surtr, Muspelheim is a land of intense heat, lava flows, and constant conflagration. Sparks from Muspelheim flew into Ginnungagap, melting the ice of Niflheim, and from this interaction, life began to emerge. During Ragnarok, the end of the world, Surtr and his fiery host will march forth from Muspelheim, wielding a flaming sword that will set the world ablaze, ultimately consuming the cosmos in fire. This realm represents the destructive and transformative power of fire, a fundamental force in Norse cosmology. It is a dangerous and uninhabitable place for beings not accustomed to extreme heat, and serves as the origin point for the sun, moon, and stars. The fiery giants residing there are formidable opponents, adding to the perilous nature of this primordial realm.
subtitle: The fiery realm of primordial chaos.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the World
keyMyths.description: Sparks from Muspelheim met the ice of Niflheim in Ginnungagap, creating Ymir and kickstarting the creation of the world.

keyMyths.title: Ragnarok
keyMyths.description: Surtr, wielding a flaming sword, leads the forces of Muspelheim to Ragnarok, setting the world ablaze and ultimately destroying it.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A primary source for Norse mythology, detailing the creation and destruction myths.
sources.text: A primary source for Norse mythology, detailing the creation and destruction myths.
